sentence: Subjective:
The patient, a 45-year-old male with a history of diabetes type two and mild intermittent asthma, presents with right elbow pain.
He reports a 3-week history of pain in the outside part of his elbow that worsens with movement and improves with rest.
The pain is described as sharp, stabbing, aching, and throbbing, with a severity of 8/10 with activity and 3/10 at rest.
He denies any trauma, fever, or other symptoms.
He has tried taking Tylenol and ibuprofen, but they provide only temporary relief.
He has a history of playing tennis regularly, which he believes may be contributing to his elbow pain.
Past medical history:
The patient has a history of diabetes type two, for which he is currently taking metformin 500mg twice daily.
He also has a history of mild intermittent asthma, for which he is taking flovent twice daily and albuterol as needed.
He has a history of an allergy to penicillin and a surgical history of a gallbladder removal 10 years ago.
Review of systems:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications:
Metformin 500mg twice daily for diabetes; Flovent twice daily for asthma; Albuterol as needed for asthma
Objective:
Vital signs:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam:

The physical exam is otherwise normal with the following exceptions: moderate lateral epicondylar tenderness of the right elbow, moderate pain with passive range of motion of the right elbow, mild pain with resisted extension of the right wrist, and a grade 3/6 systolic ejection murmur on the heart exam.
Test Results:
The right elbow x-ray shows no acute fracture or other bony abnormality, no malalignment, and no sign of joint effusion.
Assessment / Problem List:

The patient has acute lateral epicondylitis of the right elbow, also known as tennis elbow, due to overuse of the elbow; diabetes type two with blood sugar levels of 150-160 mg/dL; and mild intermittent asthma that is currently under good control.
Plan:
I recommended that the patient rest his right arm as much as possible and avoid repetitive stress in that arm for the next 4-6 weeks.
I also advised him to ice his right elbow for 20 minutes, 3 times a day, and prescribed a brace for his right forearm.
I wrote a prescription for ultram 50mg once daily for pain relief.
I also increased his metformin to 1000mg twice daily and ordered a hemoglobin A1c level, CBC, and chem-12 blood test.
I prescribed a refill of albuterol for his asthma.
Follow-up:
The patient will be seen in 4 weeks to reevaluate his elbow and adjust treatment as needed.
